name: enact-playwright
description: Browser automation tool using Playwright - captures screenshots and extracts content from web pages
capabilities: Navigate to any URL and wait for page load
Capture full-page screenshots in base64 format
Extract text content from page or specific elements
Extract HTML markup from specific DOM elements
author: EnactProtocol
securitySummary: Static scanner flagged 16 potential issues, but all findings are false positives. The code uses safe JavaScript template literals for logging (not shell backticks), empty string fields (not weak crypto), and standard fs operations for legitimate browser path detection. The skill is a standard Playwright browser automation tool with no malicious patterns.
valueStatement: Browser automation is essential for testing, data extraction, and visual verification. This skill provides a reliable way to capture screenshots and extract text or HTML content from any web page using Playwright.
userTitle: Capture screenshots and extract web content
limitations: Cannot interact with pages (no clicks, typing, or form submission)
Cannot execute JavaScript on target pages
Cannot handle authentication or login flows

useCases.title: Visual regression testing
useCases.description: Take screenshots of web pages to compare against baseline images for visual regression testing.

useCases.title: Content extraction for analysis
useCases.description: Extract text or HTML content from web pages for downstream processing or analysis.

useCases.title: Documentation and reporting
useCases.description: Capture screenshots of web pages for documentation, reports, or presentations.

promptTemplates.title: Extract text from element
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the text content from the main heading on https://example.com using the selector 'h1'

promptTemplates.title: Extract HTML content
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the HTML content of the article section from https://example.com/blog/article

promptTemplates.title: Full page screenshot with selector
promptTemplates.prompt: Take a screenshot of the product gallery on https://example.com/shop/product using selector '.product-gallery'

bestPractices: Use specific CSS selectors to target exact elements for extraction
Handle screenshots as base64-encoded strings for easy transmission
Set appropriate timeout values for slow-loading pages
Close browsers properly with try-finally blocks to prevent resource leaks
antiPatterns: Do not use this skill to scrape websites that prohibit automated access
Avoid capturing screenshots of pages with sensitive or personal information
Do not rely on this for interactive web testing that requires user simulation
Avoid extracting content from pages behind authentication without proper credentials

faq.question: What browsers are supported?
faq.answer: This skill uses Chromium via Playwright. Firefox and WebKit are not currently supported.

faq.question: Can I interact with pages (click, type)?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports navigation, screenshot capture, and content extraction. Use a different tool for interactive browser automation.

faq.question: How are screenshots delivered?
faq.answer: Screenshots are output as base64-encoded strings wrapped between SCREENSHOT_START and SCREENSHOT_END markers.

faq.question: What is the default selector?
faq.answer: The default CSS selector is 'body', which captures the entire page content.

faq.question: Can I extract content from dynamic pages?
faq.answer: This skill waits for DOM content to load but does not wait for JavaScript execution or network idle. Use the text action for static content extraction.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill runs in headless mode with no user interaction. It only navigates to URLs you specify and extracts content locally.
